title: Projects
text:
Data-driven ventures
A look at the businesses I’ve built and the decisions behind them — framed the way I approach every opportunity: research first, negotiate hard, measure everything.


S&N Supplies — Cross-Border Wholesale Distribution
Business Development Sourcing Supply Chain Market Analysis
Role: Founder & Wholesale Operations Lead · Jan 2023 – Present · Costa Mesa, CA & Dubai, UAE
Challenge. Amazon FBA sellers and retail buyers struggle to find reliable, competitively priced inventory in high-demand categories. Overstock and closeout opportunities are profitable but require constant market research to spot.
What I did. Built a global supplier network across domestic and international vendors, negotiated pricing, and coordinated procurement for name-brand health & beauty, kitchen appliances, grocery, consumer electronics, and closeout merchandise. I run continuous market and product analysis to identify high-demand categories and margin opportunities.
Result. A repeatable sourcing operation serving Amazon sellers, online entrepreneurs, and wholesale clients across two markets, with long-term partnerships built on dependable fulfillment and competitive pricing.

The category mix below reflects how I balance the portfolio between stable, repeat-demand goods and higher-margin opportunistic buys.


Code
library(ggplot2)

df <- data.frame(
  category = factor(
    c("Health &\nBeauty", "Kitchen\nAppliances", "Grocery", "Consumer\nElectronics", "Closeout /\nOverstock"),
    levels = c("Health &\nBeauty", "Kitchen\nAppliances", "Grocery", "Consumer\nElectronics", "Closeout /\nOverstock")
  ),
  share = c(28, 20, 18, 16, 18)
)
cols <- c("#0d3b45", "#1a5560", "#c07a43", "#d59a6b", "#6b7671")

ggplot(df, aes(category, share, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(share, "%")), vjust = -0.5, size = 3.4, color = "#1c2529") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 35), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Share of sourcing (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA)
  )








Figure 1: Illustrative S&N Supplies sourcing mix by category







Impact Lights — E-commerce Growth
E-commerce Digital Marketing Customer Experience
Role: Co-Founder · Mar 2021 – Present · Dubai, UAE
Challenge. Launch and grow a direct-to-consumer online retail brand from scratch in a competitive category.
What I did. Led product sourcing, digital marketing, customer experience, and daily operations. Executed data-driven campaigns across digital channels, analyzed customer feedback and sales performance to refine offerings, and managed end-to-end customer service to drive repeat business.
Result. Sustained sales growth and improved operational efficiency by continuously adapting strategy to performance insights and consumer demand.

Tracking channel performance let me shift spend toward what actually converted rather than what looked busy.


Code
library(ggplot2)

df <- data.frame(
  stage = factor(c("Reach", "Site Visits", "Add to Cart", "Purchase", "Repeat Buyer"),
                 levels = rev(c("Reach", "Site Visits", "Add to Cart", "Purchase", "Repeat Buyer"))),
  value = c(100, 42, 18, 9, 4)
)
cols <- c("#0d3b45", "#1a5560", "#2a7080", "#c07a43", "#d59a6b")

ggplot(df, aes(value, stage, fill = stage)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), hjust = -0.3, size = 3.4, color = "#1c2529") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rev(cols)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 110), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = "Relative index (Reach = 100)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA)
  )








Figure 2: Illustrative customer acquisition funnel — Impact Lights







Automated Lead-Generation System
Marketing Automation Growth Ops AI Tooling
Role: Designed & implemented for S&N Supplies
Challenge. Manual prospecting didn’t scale with the pace of the wholesale business.
What I did. Designed an automated outreach pipeline using Apollo.io for prospect data, Make.com for workflow orchestration, and AI for message drafting and qualification — turning a manual process into a repeatable, scalable system.
Result. Streamlined prospecting that frees time for high-value negotiation and partnership work.



Code
library(ggplot2)

steps <- c("Source\n(Apollo.io)", "Enrich &\nFilter", "Orchestrate\n(Make.com)", "Draft\n(AI)", "Outreach &\nReply")
df <- data.frame(x = seq_along(steps), y = 0, num = seq_along(steps), label = steps)
arrows <- data.frame(x = head(df$x, -1) + 0.25, xend = head(df$x, -1) + 0.75, y = 0, yend = 0)

ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = arrows, aes(x = x, xend = xend, y = y, yend = yend),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
               color = "#c07a43", linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(data = df, aes(x, y), size = 16, color = "#0d3b45") +
  geom_text(data = df, aes(x, y, label = num), color = "white", size = 4.2) +
  geom_text(data = df, aes(x, y - 0.22, label = label), size = 3, color = "#1c2529", vjust = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.5, length(steps) + 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.6, 0.4)) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA))








Figure 3: Lead-generation pipeline stages






Charts above are illustrative and use representative figures to demonstrate the analytical approach behind each venture.

title: Saad Al Awadhi.
text:

About

title: Saad Al Awadhi.
text:
Building value across global markets
Entrepreneur, operator, and business professional with hands-on experience leading cross-border sourcing, wholesale distribution, and e-commerce operations. I founded S&N Supplies, a global sourcing business connecting Amazon sellers, e-commerce brands, and retail buyers with reliable products, competitive pricing, and scalable procurement.
My background combines operational execution with strategic business development — supplier negotiations, market analysis, international trade, partnership development, and growth strategy. Currently completing a BBA in Marketing Management at California State Polytechnic University, Pomona, where I focus on data-driven decision-making, consumer insights, and modern marketing strategy applied to real business growth.


Business Development
Client acquisition, strategic partnerships, and growth strategy across the US and UAE.


Sourcing & Supply Chain
Vendor negotiations, wholesale procurement, and international trade operations.


Marketing Analytics
Market research, consumer behavior, and data-driven campaign optimization.

title: Saad Al Awadhi.
text:
Highlighted achievements

Founded and scaled S&N Supplies, a US–UAE wholesale distribution business serving Amazon FBA sellers and retail buyers.
Designed an automated lead-generation and outreach system (Apollo.io + Make.com + AI) to support scalable prospecting.
Co-founded Impact Lights, an e-commerce brand grown from startup through growth stages via data-driven digital campaigns.
Completed UAE National Service with distinction (12/12) and a perfect 5/5 physical fitness grade.
First-place finishes in local equestrian competitions in Dubai — discipline and performance under pressure.
